mnemonics: S.O.P. (Simplex, Optimization, Problems) - Remember the core method and its purpose for solving linear programming problems.
keyPoints: The Simplex Method is an algorithm for solving linear programming problems by moving along the edges of the feasible region to find the optimal vertex.
Duality Theory states that every linear programming problem has an associated dual problem, and the solutions of these problems are closely related.
Sensitivity Analysis examines how changes in the coefficients (cost, constraints, resources) affect the optimal solution.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to convert inequality constraints into equations by adding slack, surplus, or artificial variables when setting up the initial Simplex tableau.
Misinterpreting the signs of the coefficients in the objective row (indicators) when determining the entering and leaving variables.
Failing to check for special cases like degeneracy, multiple optimal solutions, or an unbounded feasible region.
